In order to understand why Kamyaar is doing what he is doing, you have to understand psychology.  His dad had a one time affair and his mom continued to punish him for 15 years.  She could have left, but she stayed and held that over him, punishing him daily.  No peace in the house was normal to Kamyaar and he is doing the same.  He feels Ayra didn't trust him  and hurt him badly and now he's with her but punishing her too.  To him, punishment is the solution to healing.  He also resorted to what he is familiar with and feels most comfortable with which is his previous life of  partying, drinking, etc.  and being around particular friends.  Try breaking a habit, it's hard and we tend to resort back to our old ways easily even if it's bad for us.   I still don't understand why everyone isn't holding Faria and Khawaar more accountable when the topic comes up and how Khawaars mother had the audacity to say what she said at the celebration dinner to Ayra..  Her son was the reason behind Ayra and Kamyaars relationship breaking down but I guess Pakistanis will be Pakistani- no self reflection!

One of the sensible post I saw on Facebook, raised by a sensible person. Do not know why everyone is showing sympathy to that Ayra, she never tried to talk nicely with Kamyar and never said sorry genuinely once. Everyone is focusing on Kamyar’s normal behavior with Faria (even he didn't forgive her, and insulted her many times), but that doesn’t justify Ayra’s character. If the writer had explored Kamyar’s psychological side, the audience would have understood why he was with Faria.Not addressing this aspect made many people without any knowledge of psychology interpret things as they pleased, which weakened his character in their eyes.

Almost all Ayra’s actions have been aimed at insulting Kamyar. The writer made it seem like she wronged Khawar and went to apologize to him, but it was clear she was upset that he brought Khawar to her house in the car while she was talking to Naveen. She wasn’t upset about what Khawar did to Kamyar. Even when she slapped him, it was because of what he did to the girl in the video, not because of Kamyar.

It doesn't make Kamyar right for associating with Faria again. But Faria means nothing to him whereas Ayra is and was his everything! We often don't expect outsiders to go miles for us but we expect simple, small efforts from our loved ones. 

All he needs at the moment is a plain, simple ,heartfelt apology which never came from Ayra's side. I feel the boy would have given the marriage a chance if there was a proper apology. And we are easily pushing his heartbreak under the rug. Trust once destroyed by the most closest ones break us more than we can imagine and it often leaves us with a lifelong scar. You cannot see the person same as before, ever again.

That’s literally all there is to it. Instead, all we saw was that ridiculous dinner table scene.. her insulting him, mocking him in front of his parents, disrespecting him, and saying to your husband, "Here’s your coffee. I won’t bring it next time with a disgusted expression". And okay, so the scene at the dining table where everyone expected kamyar to act like a normal family with them was insane. all kamyar's life his parents kept fighting and ruined his life in the midst of it, gave him absolutely zero family structure and stability and then one fine day after more than 2 decades of this conflict, they decide to start loving each other and act like a normal family and expect kamyar to forget about all the years of trauma he went through and sit with them and be the perfect loving caring son??? and ayra demanding him to act this way instead of understanding his trauma is even more insane, cant believe an insensitive person like her is a doctor. they need to add mortality tests to the mdcat thing at this point.

Do feminists even know what's the meaning of "misogyny"? They make me feel like he’s physically abusing her as a husband? Oh come on, who was the one being disrespected, humiliated, and belittled by her throughout the drama? He’s just deeply hurt by her and wants to make her realize how badly she’s treated him all along.

But everything becomes pink and normal as long as the parents solved their problems.Yet Ayra, the ‘therapist,’ couldn’t even say these words to her husband. Instead, she just says, 'Don’t expect special treatment, take your coffee, and I won’t bring it next time.' Is it normal for a wife to treat her husband like this in the first place? Is it normal that someone who said she entered the marriage out of guilt shows no sense of guilt at all, but only disrespect for her husband? Complete nonsense.

If people are still justifying Ayra’s character, and see that's "strong independent", they obviously need help.

Why’s everyone against Kamyar? He just spoke the truth. She insulted him for so long, and no one said a word, but now "Khawar" is better? Lol. When Khawar lectured her on “respect by saying, I can't imagine how Kamyar is treating you behind closed doors?, why didn’t she shut him down properly? But "thanks for your concern”with meaningless expression?

And Kamyar was rightly insulting Khawar’s mother, so why did Ayra get butthurt and ask him not to create a scene? Kamyar showed Ayra the mirror. Ayra is no different from those who’ve wronged him, yet she refuses to admit it and still paints him as the bad one. The same foolishness happened when she tried to stop Kamyar while Khawar was disrespecting him… she was giving him lessons about controlling his anger. God help any man with a wife like that.

I don't think writer tried to show Misunderstanding in the way you all are thinking. It's about misbehaving with Ayra. Khawar will simply say Ayra shouldn't live with you she is scared of you. I am here to take her with me. Kamyar question will be what is he doing in his room? That's the big problem for Ayra or for any husband/wife. Remember Ayra got angry when she saw Faria in the room.

It’s as if the drama wanted to prove how much Kamyar loves and trusts her, but at the expense of his own feelings. It would have been stronger and more powerful if Ayra had felt the same pain and betrayal much earlier, not just in the final episodes.

What I’m about to say may annoy many people, but it’s the truth everyone seems unwilling to acknowledge: the biggest problem in the story was never Kamyar.. It was Ayra.

From the very beginning, Kamyar was the one who faced accusations, judgment, and humiliation, yet we never truly saw Ayra acknowledge her mistakes or take real responsibility for her actions. Instead of discussing this, people often resort to ready-made excuses: “she’s a female character written by a man,” or claiming that viewers only sympathize with Kamyar because they *like the actor*.

The writer is trying, through this to show the difference in the nature of the relationship between the two characters: Kamyar loves madly and trusts Ayra deeply even in difficult situations. When he will see her with Khawar, his reaction will not be like hers during the video incident because he trusts in his love! Meanwhile, Ayra is not always confident in herself or in her love to the same extent, which highlights the depth and steadiness of his love compared to her hesitation, insecure or restraint.

This is the parallel the writer wants to draw! 

This is the difference between them (Their love!) 

This is Kamyar’s love and trust story, and this reality check will finally make Ayra realize and mature, so their relationship will deepen… I think this is a good trope for the story, and it will be heartwarming! 

But some will say: "

Khawar’s character is well-known to Kamyar and Ayra. Ayra doesn’t pay him any attention, so why would anyone assume she’s being misunderstood here? Kamyar isn’t different in this situation. No one could have doubted Ayra. In Kamyar’s video incident, it was different.. he was with a stranger. His reputation and image were already such that Ayra misinterpreted it, which was somewhat justified, but not in the case of Khawar and Ayra! And this isn’t a parallel?" 

But my response is… 

how can this be considered normal? In real life, it would be impossible for a husband to act naturally unless he were completely rational and had absolute trust in his wife. How did a stranger like Khawar even get into the house when there were guards? Let alone reach their bedroom unless she herself allowed him in! We all know it’s Faria.. She does whatever she wants in the palace, and no one stops her; everyone knows her connection to the family. Any husband in Kamyar’s situation wouldn’t have known who let him in, yet he still found him in his bedroom with his wife. This is also the reason why I think Ayra was laying on top of him while rubbing her hands on his biceps (Hania here looks like a cat rubbing against its master to win his favor, tease him, and pamper him! It's almost like she's calming him down or she just wanted to feel his warmth cause come on they are husband and wife and both love each other.. So, naturally, they would want to spend at least a little time together.

Honestly, for everyone moaning about the writer — I think they’ve done a brilliant job. This is a realistic portrayal of a wounded person whose parents weren’t emotionally available. It shows exactly how people project their wounds onto the wrong person. In real life, men and women often carry unresolved emotional pain and make poor choices, like seeking validation or sexual satisfaction from someone toxic, while blaming someone else entirely. That’s what’s happening here — the guy is still carrying on with the woman who enables him, while unfairly blaming the girl who rejected him. Stop complaining and appreciate the psychological realism the writer is showing
